heading: Wavell Heights vs Wealwandangie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wavell Heights, Queensland and Wealwandangie, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wealwandangie has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to Wavell Heights (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wavell Heights or Wealwandangie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wavell Heights has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Wealwandangie scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wavell Heights scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Wealwandangie scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wavell Heights or Wealwandangie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wealwandangie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
